criteria.name: Probabilistic Method
criteria.evidence: uncertainty.py::_distribution_samples uses scipy.stats.gaussian_kde to produce a 256-sample distribution from observation-derived station-reference divergence values. The KDE output is clipped and normalized, then used to derive both the point estimate (mean of samples) and confidence intervals. Not a scalar heuristic — a full distributional resampling.

criteria.name: Station-Removal Risk
criteria.evidence: quantify_station_removal_risk produces per-station risk_probability, ci_lower, ci_upper, and risk_band. When benchmark contains raw 'observations' (from benchmark_to_stanhope which computes |station - reference| absolute deltas on real PEINP data), those are used directly. Falls back to documented synthetic generation only when observations are absent.

criteria.name: Confidence Intervals
criteria.evidence: CI computed as np.quantile(samples, [0.1, 0.9]) from the KDE-resampled distribution. Test test_quantify_station_removal_risk_intervals_widen_with_less_overlap verifies strict ordering: alpha (overlap=120) width < beta (72) width < gamma (12) width, confirming intervals widen with less data.

criteria.name: Assumptions and Limitations
criteria.evidence: Each output row includes 'assumptions' (documenting gaussian_kde usage and synthetic fallback) and 'limitations' (flagging overlap_count < 24 as insufficient). _limitations() function returns specific text. Tests verify 'insufficient' for gamma (12 overlaps) and 'adequate' for alpha (120 overlaps). Assumptions also surfaced in function docstrings.

criteria.name: Integration with Recommendations
criteria.evidence: redundancy.py::build_station_recommendations calls quantify_station_removal_risk, merges results with benchmark, and uses risk_band and ci_upper in _recommendation_from_row to determine keep/remove/defer (high band or ci_upper>=0.7 → keep; correlation>=0.95 and low band → remove; else defer). Recommendation output includes risk_probability, ci_lower, ci_upper, risk_band, and evidence string referencing uncertainty.

criteria.name: Test Quality
criteria.evidence: 8 tests all pass. Tests verify distributional behavior: KDE output shape (512 samples), std > 0, mean ordering preservation, quantile proximity to empirical values within 0.2 tolerance. Real PEINP CSVs loaded in _real_observation_benchmark and test_distribution_samples_follow_empirical_delta_distribution. Synthetic fixture in _sample_similarity_frame is documented with justification. No existence-only or type-only checks.

summary: PASS — all 6 acceptance criteria for Phase 6 (Interpret) are satisfied. uncertainty.py uses scipy.stats.gaussian_kde to produce distributional risk estimates per station, with confidence intervals that widen appropriately with less overlap. Assumptions and limitations are surfaced per-station. Recommendations in redundancy.py integrate uncertainty estimates directly into keep/remove/defer logic. Tests verify distributional properties (shape, ordering, quantile fidelity) using both real PEINP data and documented synthetic fixtures. All 8 tests pass.
